section_name: Waiver of Fees for Foster Child or Youth, Homeless Child or Youth, or Victim or Child of Victim of Dating or Family Violence

subsections.text: A person is exempt from the payment of any fee for the issuance of a driver’s license, as provided under this chapter, if that person is:
younger than 18 years of age and in the managing conservatorship of the Department of Family and Protective Services;
at least 18 years of age, but younger than 21 years of age, and resides in a foster care placement, the cost of which is paid by the Department of Family and Protective Services;
a homeless child or youth as defined by 42 U.S.C. Section 11434a;
a victim of dating violence as defined by Section 71.0021 (Dating Violence) , Family Code;
a victim of family violence as defined by Section 51.002 (Definitions) , Human Resources Code; or
a child of a victim described by Subdivision (4) or (5).

history.raw: Acts 2021, 87th Leg., R.S., Ch. 374 (S.B.798), Sec. 3, eff. September 1, 2021.
